title: Oxybaphus hirsutus
intro: Oxybaphus hirsutus, also known as hairy four o'clock, is a species of flowering plant in the four o'clock family (Nyctaginaceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Bioactive constituents of Oxybaphus hirsutus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The hairy four o'clock plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves to promote wound healing and skin health.
taxonomy: Oxybaphus hirsutus is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family, known as Oenotheraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The genus Oxybaphus is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as desert five-spot. Oxybaphus hirsutus is often classified as a perennial herb that produces small white or pink flowers. This species is also known as hairy desert five-spot due to its hairy stems and leaves.
